goal: Provide both a production-faithful UI Lab workbench and a folder-first UI-variant standard in which collaborators can replace the complete employee experience behind a neutral capability model without inheriting the production Web client's visual grammar.
user_outcome: A collaborator can run one command, create or open apps/web/ui-variants/<variant-slug>/, work with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or no agent inside that bounded folder, see the exact variant against deterministic employee scenarios, validate it, and preserve production employee capabilities without preserving the Web client's layout, terminology, DOM hierarchy, or component model.
hard_constraints: Use the actual Next.js Web application and production employee data contracts.
Keep the production Web client available only as an optional reference-client capability slot.
Project payloads into the neutral EmployeeExperienceModelV1 contract before variant rendering.
Do not require variants to preserve production appearance, layout, navigation, terminology, DOM hierarchy, or component model.
Do not allow variant imports to escape their folder or reach private application modules.
Do not allow direct network access, ambient browser storage, undeclared dependencies, Node modules, Workers, or WASM.
Generate a static registry with literal lazy imports so Next.js can discover and split every checked-in variant.
Do not include or depend on a coding agent.
Do not require a production build for the normal edit-preview loop.
Do not persist promoted designs that cannot be reproduced from source control.
Do not expose repository file-write APIs outside explicit loopback development mode.
Do not add Storybook as a parallel primary application.
Do not add a database migration or runtime service for source-controlled UI variants or presets.
Keep UI Lab unavailable in normal production-like environments.
initial_hypothesis: Use Next development mode as the live compiler; retain the production-component workbench for strategy and preset work; add a sibling folder-first variant product line with a neutral capability contract, generated literal lazy registry, containment host, bounded intent bridge, deterministic fixture routes, folder-local agent guidance, and an executable doctor that rejects boundary violations.

evidence_boundary.compiled_browser: not yet established by a headed all-variant desktop/mobile run
evidence_boundary.visual_quality_judgment: human review remains required even when automated vectors pass

documentation_reconciliation.changes_after_verification_basis: machine-readable evidence and affected-file ledgers only
